qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing default ReadTimeout enables slow POST denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns Caddy HTTP server timeout defaults in modules/caddyhttp/app.go and a slow POST body-read denial of service. The advisory concerns FastCGI split_path handling where splitPos() computes a byte index on strings.ToLower(path) and buildEnv() applies that index to the original Unicode path to derive SCRIPT_NAME, SCRIPT_FILENAME, and PATH_INFO. The vulnerable component, function, control flow, and root cause do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described FastCGI Unicode case-folding length expansion bug in splitPos() and buildEnv(). The only qualifying finding identifies a separate Caddy HTTP server missing ReadTimeout slow POST denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5r3v-vc8m-m96g describes github.com/caddyserver/caddy/modules/caddyhttp/fastcgi.Transport.splitPos() computing a split index on strings.ToLower(path) and buildEnv() slicing the original path to derive SCRIPT_NAME, SCRIPT_FILENAME, and PATH_INFO.

missing_read_timeout_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies modules/caddyhttp/app.go timeout default handling, specifically that ReadHeaderTimeout and IdleTimeout receive defaults while ReadTimeout remains zero.
The qualifying finding does not mention FastCGI, splitPos(), Unicode case folding, SCRIPT_NAME, SCRIPT_FILENAME, PATH_INFO, or the path-confusion source-to-sink path described by the advisory.
Per the PoC execution rule, no PoC was executed because there is no advisory-matched qualifying finding.
